title: Marsdenia dissitiflora
intro: Marsdenia dissitiflora, also known as the Himalayan milkvine, is a perennial vine native to the Himalayan region, characterized by its climbing habit and showy, white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Marsdenia dissitiflora include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder, and is commonly used to make herbal teas, capsules, and tablets.
taxonomy: Marsdenia dissitiflora belongs to the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family is part of the order Gentianales, which is a group of flowering plants. The order Gentianales is further classified under the clade asterids and the larger clade angiosperms. Angiosperms are a group of plants that produce flowers and seeds enclosed within the flower. Marsdenia dissitiflora's classification is a part of the broader plant taxonomy system.
